title: Podman remote Windows client asks for absolute volume paths regardless of input
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When running the Podman Windows client against a WSL 2 remote `podman run -v` will tell me to provide absolute paths, no matter what I provide.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

While struggling with #11386, I'm running a Podman service in a WSL 2 Ubuntu host through

```
$ sudo podman system service -t 0 tcp:0.0.0.0:2566
```

Then, using the Podman remote Windows client, I can do things like

```
>podman run -it debian /bin/bash
```

and it just works, so that's great.

However, one I start using `-v`, I'll run into error messages

```
>podman run -v .:/app -it debian /bin/bash
Error: invalid container path "/app", must be an absolute path
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Since we're running remotely, I'm not sure exactly what I would expect for the host directory part; probably something in the WSL 2 host. What I would expect is the error that the container directory part must be an absolute path regardless of what I put in: `/app` seems like it would fit the description of "absolute".

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

comments.body: @flouthoc I think you fixed that already on main, right?
@Luap99 I think this is clone of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10900 this is fixed in latest main. @fuglede Could you please try latest main ?
